source_pr.title: tests: improve timing robustness in TestStaleReadAllCombinations
source_pr.body:
### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #63271

Problem Summary:

The `TestStaleReadAllCombinations/tidb_read_staleness` test case is flaky under high CI load due to tight timing constraints.

### What changed and how does it work?

**Changes made:**

1. Relax the time constraints
2. Reorder cases, to run `tidb_read_staleness` cases first 

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Side effects
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory  
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation
- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: The `TestStaleReadAllCombinations/tidb_read_staleness` test case is flaky under high CI load due to tight timing constraints.
failure_signature: The `TestStaleReadAllCombinations/tidb_read_staleness` test case is flaky under high CI load due to tight timing constraints.
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky due to timing-sensitive asynchronous behavior (background tasks/events not fully settled). Fixed sleeps are brittle under CI timing jitter. This PR stabilizes it by: **Changes made:**.

evidence: evidence: The `TestStaleReadAllCombinations/tidb_read_staleness` test case is flaky under high CI load due to tight timing constraints.
